SANCTION OF GRAM PANCHAYAT BUILDINGS

*421. SHRI BAJRANG MANOHAR SONWANE:
SHRI PRAVEEN PATEL:
Will the Minister of PANCHAYATI RAJ be pleased to state:
(a) the criteria and guidelines followed for sanctioning Gram Panchayat Bhawans;
(b) the number of Gram Panchayat Bhawans/buildings sanctioned by the
Government duringFinancial Year 2024-25 till February 2025, including
Jalgaon, Phulpur, Shahdol and Balaghat Parliamentary Constituencies of
Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh respectively along with
timeline for completion of the Gram Panchayat Bhawans/Buildings, State and
district-wise;
(c) the details of percentage of Gram Panchayat offices/Bhawans/Buildings that
have been computerized as on February 2025 and the steps taken for
computerization of Gram Panchayat offices/Bhawans/Buildings during the said
α The sign + marked above the name of a Member indicates that the Question was actually asked on the floor of the House
by that Member.

period, State and district-wise particularly in Bhiwani-Mahendergarh
Parliamentary constituency and aspirational district of Sirohi in Rajasthan and
tribal areas;
(d) the number of Gram-Panchayats covered under the Special Gram Sabha cum-
Orientation Programme and the details of its impact on rural development,
particularly in Uttar Pradesh and Sidhi Parliamentary Constituency in Madhya
Pradesh along with all the Gram Panchayats of Udaipur Parliamentary
Constituency, State/district-wise;
(e) the main subjects on which Panchayati Raj Representatives have been trained
during such training programme and the manner in which it has improved the
administrative efficiency atthe village level along with the details of areas
focused upon in the said training programmes;
(f) the role played by the young fellows and elected representatives associated
with Panchayati Raj in this initiative and the contribution there of in making
PRRs a success; and
(g) the details of new initiatives for Digital Panchayat, e-Governance and
enhancing transparency along with new schemes to strengthen self-
governance in rural areas and enhance financial independence of
Panchayats?

 (cid:250)ाम पंचायत िवकास योजना (जीपीडीपी),

 एक पेड़ मा ँ के नाम।

(च): मं(cid:253)ालय क(cid:236) पहल के तहत देश भर म (cid:164) (cid:250)ाम पंचायत(cid:332) के कुछ समूह(cid:332) म (cid:164) यवु ा फे लो को शािमल िकया

मदद करते ह(cid:167)। उनक(cid:236) सि(cid:248)य भागीदारी से िवशेष (cid:250)ाम सभा सह-(cid:255)बोधन काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म(cid:332) क(cid:236) गणु व(cid:176)ा और

(छ): रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय (cid:250)ाम (cid:214)वराज अिभयान (आरजीएसए) क(cid:236) संशोिधत योजना के िमशन मोड प(cid:229)रयोजना

सेवा (cid:255)दायगी , पारदिश(cid:170)ता, जवाबदेही और पंचायत शासन म(cid:164) (cid:255)भावशीलता सिु नि(cid:306)त करन े म(cid:164) सहायता

वािष(cid:170)क पंचायत िवकास योजनाए ं (जीपीडीपी) ऑनलाइन तैयार करना और अपलोड िकया जाना तथा
स(cid:211)ु यवि(cid:214)थत िव(cid:176)ीय लेनदेन और पारदश(cid:234) खरीद के िलए साव(cid:170)जिनक िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:255)बंधन (cid:255)णाली
(पीएफएमएस) और सरकारी ई-माक(cid:165) ट(cid:200)लेस (जीईएम) के साथ एक(cid:236)करण संभव ह(cid:242)आ है।
ई-(cid:250)ाम(cid:214)वराज िवकास संबंधी गितिविधय(cid:332) और िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:211)यय को वा(cid:214)तिवक समय पर (cid:367)ैक करन े

लाभकारी है। यह काय(cid:170)-आधा(cid:229)रत लेखांकन को सरल बनाता है, सटीक िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:229)रपोिट(cid:171)ग के िलए
साव(cid:170)जिनक िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:255)बंधन (cid:255)णाली (PFMS) के साथ एक(cid:236)कृत करता है, और प(cid:229)रसंपि(cid:176)य(cid:332) क(cid:236) िजयो-
टैिगंग सिहत प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंक(cid:236) (cid:211)यापक िनगरानी क(cid:236) अनमु ित देता है।
इसके अलावा, मं(cid:253)ालय (cid:300)ारा िवकिसत िकए गए एि(cid:200)लके शन जैस े िक मेरी पंचायत ऐप, पंचायत
क(cid:236) गितिविधय(cid:332) पर मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170) जानकारी क(cid:236) पह(cid:242)चँ जनता तक (cid:255)दान करके पारदिश(cid:170)ता को बढ़ावा देते ह(cid:167),

जवाबदेही को मजबूत बनाने और (cid:214)थानीय शासन म(cid:164) सूिचत नाग(cid:229)रक भागीदारी को (cid:255)ो(cid:194)सािहत करन े म(cid:164)

(cid:250)ाम सभाओ ंके संचालन म(cid:164) पारदिश(cid:170)ता लाना और बेहतर (cid:255)बंधन करना है।

DR. T. SUMATHY ALIAS THAMIZHACHI THANGAPANDIAN: Hon. Speaker Sir,
as per the 2022-23 data from the Ministry, out of 2,71,770 Gram Panchayats across
the country, 51,508 do not have their own building. In contrast, Tamil Nadu, under
the leadership of our Chief Minister Thalapathy M.K. Stalin, has allocated Rs.1,000
crore under the Mudhalvarin Grama Salaigal Membattu Thittam for improving the
rural roads and infrastructure with a target of upgrading 10,000 kilometres of roads
by 2025-26. I would like to ask the hon. Minister that despite the Union
Government’s scheme supplementing the State efforts why there has been a failure
to ensure equitable infrastructure development for Gram Panchayats nationwide.
And, what steps are being taken to bridge this rural governance gap?
(cid:255)ो. एस. पी. िसहं बघेल : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) जी, मूल (cid:255)(cid:307) (cid:250)ाम (cid:214)वराज अिभयान का है और माननीय सद(cid:214)या
उसको (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी (cid:250)ामीण सड़क योजना क(cid:236) तरफ ले जा रही ह(cid:167) । जहां तक पंचायत भवन का मामला है
तो पंचायत भवन के िलए रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) को िलिमटेड (cid:214)तर पर के (cid:198)(cid:254) के (cid:300)ारा पैसा िदया जाता है । िजन पंचायत(cid:332)
क(cid:236) आबादी तीन हजार है और संतृ(cid:302) नह(cid:233) हो पाए ह(cid:167), उसके ऊपर ह(cid:167), उनके िलए रा(cid:186)य क(cid:236) िडमा(cid:193)ड पर
20 लाख (cid:321)पये पंचायत भवन के िलए और उसी कै (cid:204)पस म(cid:164) जन सिु वधा के (cid:198)(cid:254) खलु सके , उसके िलए पांच
लाख (cid:321)पये का (cid:255)ावधान िकया गया है ।

CAPITAL INVESTMENT IN AGRICULTURE
*422. SHRI PARSHOTTAMBHAI RUPALA:

state:
(a) The current level of capital investment in agriculture, including the allocation of
funds for irrigation, fertilizers,and other inputs;
(b) the measures taken by the Government to enhance private capital investment
in agriculture, including the provision of incentives and subsidies;
(c) the details of the schemes and programs implemented by the Government to
promote agricultural investment, including the Agriculture Infrastructure Fund and
the Farmers’ Welfare Scheme; and
(d) the allocation of funds for agricultural investment in the current financial year?

21933.50 करोड़ था। यह 2024-25 म (cid:164) 5.98 गनु ा से अिधक बढ़कर 131195.21 करोड़ (cid:321)पये हो
गया है। इसके अलावा, भारत सरकार िविभ(cid:198)न क(cid:164) (cid:254) (cid:255)ायोिजत योजनाओ ंके मा(cid:197)यम से उिचत नीितगत

के सफल काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन के प(cid:229)रणाम(cid:214)व(cid:322)प देश म(cid:164) खा(cid:299)ा(cid:198)न उ(cid:194)पादन (cid:229)रकॉड(cid:170) (cid:214)तर पर पह(cid:242)ंच गया है, जो
2013-14 म(cid:164) 263.5 एमएमटी से बढ़कर 2024-25 म(cid:164) 331 एमएमटी हो गया है।
जहां तक उव(cid:170)रक सि(cid:202)सडी के िलए िनिध आवंटन का (cid:255)(cid:307) है, उव(cid:170)रक िवभाग ने िव(cid:176) वष(cid:170) 2024-
25 के दौरान 1,77,129.50 करोड़ (cid:321)पये आवंिटत िकए ह(cid:167)।

जल संसाधन, नदी िवकास और गंगा संर(cid:177)ण िवभाग, जल शि(cid:265) मं(cid:253)ालय, पीएमके एसवाई के

28 फरवरी 2025 तक एआईएफ के तहत 98,744 प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंके िलए 59,943 करोड़
(cid:321)पये मंजूर िकए गए ह(cid:167)। इन (cid:214)वीकृत प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंसे कृिष (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) 96,680 करोड़ (cid:321)पये का िनवेश
जुटाया गया है। एआईएफ के तहत (cid:214)वीकृत (cid:255)मुख प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंम(cid:164) 25,671 क(cid:214)टम हाय(cid:229)रंग स(cid:164)टर,

SHRI LAVU SRIKRISHNA DEVARAYALU: Sir, in the answer given by the Minister
regarding Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana, the focus of this Scheme is,
01.04.2025 57
as he has just mentioned, for providing support to fisheries entrepreneurs, fish
farmers, fishermen, and all related stakeholders. So, this is a Scheme focused on
fisheries entrepreneurs. There is an increase of allocation by Rs. 2,300 core. This
allocation is 56 per cent more than the allocation of Rs. 1,500 crore made during
the year 2023-24. So, the focus is on the farmer. But, if you look at the allocation
and spending, in the last year Rs. 1,500 core have been allocated, but most of the
spending is being done to the State Government instead of farmers.
I asked the farmers, what is the reason behind this? The majority of them
mentioned that the fish farmers do not do one acre or twp acres, they do 10 acres
to 12 acres. The cost of input itself goes to almost Rs. 80 lakh to Rs. 90 lakh. But,
individual farmers can only get Rs. 5 lakh per farmer. So, the amount that has been
allocated for each farmer for improving the technology in the fish farms is very less
than what has been mentioned in the Scheme.

irrigation, Namo Drone Didi, and other things which will increase technology in the
farming sector. I would like to know from the Minister whether in the same way the
Ministry is also looking in a direction whereby more farmers can actually take this
Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana funding and increase the productivity in
their farm rather than the State Government using it.

(क): (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी अनसु ूिचत जाित अ(cid:203)यदु य योजना (पीएम-अजय) के आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घटक के अंतग(cid:170)त, वष(cid:170)
2011 क(cid:236) जनगणना के अनसु ार 40% से अिधक अनुसूिचत जाित क(cid:236) आबादी वाले और कुल 500 या
उससे अिधक क(cid:236)जनसं(cid:180)या वाले गांव चयन के िलए पा(cid:253) ह(cid:167)। वष(cid:170) 2018-19 से अब तक 29,847 गांव(cid:332)
को िचि(cid:313)त िकया गया है, िजनम(cid:164) से 11,076 गांव(cid:332) को आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घोिषत िकया गया है। िव(cid:176)ीय वष(cid:170)
2024-25 के दौरान, रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य-(cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म (cid:164) 4991 गांव(cid:332) को आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घोिषत िकया गया है।
महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) म(cid:164) आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घटक के तहत 515 गांव(cid:332) को िचि(cid:313)त िकया गया है और 260 गांव(cid:332) को आदश(cid:170)
(cid:250)ाम घोिषत िकया गया है, िजनम(cid:164) से वष(cid:170) 2024-25 के दौरान महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) म(cid:164) 46 गांव(cid:332) को आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घोिषत

गया है। वष(cid:170) 2024-25 के दौरान,पीएम-अजय के आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घटक के तहत अब तक रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य-

(i) क(cid:164) (cid:254), रा(cid:186)य, िजला और (cid:250)ाम (cid:214)तर पर िविभ(cid:198)न सिमितयां काय(cid:170)रत ह(cid:167), जैस-े (cid:250)ाम (cid:214)तरीय अिभसरण

(एसएलसीसी) और क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय (cid:214)तर पर प(cid:229)रयोजना मू(cid:208)यांकन सह अिभसरण सिमित (पीएसीसी) जो योजना

(cid:174)ी गज(cid:198)े (cid:254) िसहं पटेल : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) जी, म (cid:167) आपके मा(cid:197)यम से, सदन को भी यह सं(cid:178)ान म (cid:164) रह े िक
जो (cid:255)धान मं(cid:253)ी अनसु ूिचत जाित अ(cid:203)यदु य योजना है, वह अनसु ूिचत जाित वग(cid:170) के समुदाय के िवकास के
िलए चलाई जा रही है । इसम(cid:164) इस समुदाय के बालक-बािलकाओ ंके छा(cid:253)ावास और गांव के उ(cid:194)थान के
िलए यह योजना संचािलत है । म(cid:167) माननीय मं(cid:253)ी जी से यह जानना चाहता ह(cid:243)ं िक म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश रा(cid:186)य म (cid:164)
‘पीएम-अजय योजना’ म(cid:164) अनसु ूिचत जाित वग(cid:170) के िकतन े गांव(cid:332) को शािमल िकया गया है? कौन से आदश(cid:170)
(cid:250)ाम ह(cid:167), उसम(cid:164) (cid:179)या-(cid:179)या अनदु ान सहायता (cid:255)ा(cid:302) हो रही है? इस योजना के मा(cid:197)यम से बालक-बािलकाओ ं
के िलए िकतने छा(cid:253)ावास संचािलत हो रहे ह(cid:167)?
(cid:174)ी रामदास अठावले : अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, यह योजना वष(cid:170) 2021-22 म(cid:164) श(cid:322)ु ह(cid:242)ई थी । जो आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम
योजना है, यह योजना इसिलए शु(cid:322) क(cid:236) थी िक पहले क(cid:236) सरकार(cid:332) ने अनुसूिचत जनजाित के िलए कुछ
नह(cid:233) िकया था । जो (cid:255)धान मं(cid:253)ी अनसूिचत जाित अ(cid:203)यदयु योजना है, इसम(cid:164) आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम भी होता है,
अनदु ान सहायता दी जाती है और छा(cid:253)ावास भी बनाया जाता है । यह योजना वष(cid:170) 2021-22 म(cid:164) श(cid:322)ु ह(cid:242)ई
थी और उसम(cid:164) आइड(cid:164)िटफाई िकए गए गांव 29,847 ह(cid:167) । गांव क(cid:236) जो पॉपलु शे न है, वह (cid:186)यादा होनी चािहए
और शेड्यू(cid:208)ड का(cid:214)ट क(cid:236) पॉपलु शे न 40 परस(cid:164)ट होनी चािहए ।
इस योजना के तहत 20 लाख (cid:321)पए िदये जाते ह(cid:167) । इस योजना के तहत रोड, (cid:369)ेनेज, इलेि(cid:179)(cid:367)िसटी
पो(cid:208)स आिद के काम आते ह(cid:167) । आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम के तहत 11,076 गांव ह(cid:167) । इसम(cid:164) हमने 1,698 करोड़ (cid:321)पए
खच(cid:170) िकये ह(cid:167) । वष(cid:170) 2024-25 म(cid:164), आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम के तहत 4,991 गांव(cid:332) को िड(cid:179)लेयर िकया गया है । महारा(cid:213)(cid:367)

म(cid:164), आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम के तहत 515 िवलेजेज आइड(cid:164)िटफाई िकये गये ह(cid:167) ।… ((cid:211)यवधान)
म(cid:167) ये महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) के िलए बता रहा ह(cid:243) ँ ।… ((cid:211)यवधान) महोदय, (cid:255)(cid:307) महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) के जलगांव से संबंिधत है ।
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) : माननीय सद(cid:214)य म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश से सद(cid:214)य ह(cid:167) ।
(cid:174)ी रामदास अठावले : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) जी, म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश म(cid:164) आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम(cid:332) क(cid:236) स(cid:180)ं या 1,690 है । वहा ँ
घोिषत िकये गये आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम(cid:332) क(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या 1,223 है । इसके िलए हमने 270.74 करोड़ (cid:321)पए द े िदए ह(cid:167) ।
हमने 5,299 काय(cid:170) पूर े िकये ह(cid:167) । इसम(cid:164) 11 लाख 5 हजार लोग(cid:332) को फायदा ह(cid:242)आ है ।
आपने जो पूछा है, इसम(cid:164) म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश म(cid:164) छा(cid:253)ावास(cid:332) क(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या 86 है, महारा(cid:213)(cid:367) म(cid:164) 59 है, गजु रात
म(cid:164) 17 ह(cid:167) । इसी तरह से, हम छा(cid:253)ावास(cid:332) के िलए अनुदान दते े ह(cid:167) और हम छा(cid:253)ावास(cid:332) के िलए काम करते
ह(cid:167) ।
(cid:174)ी गजे(cid:198)(cid:254) िसहं पटेल : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) जी, म (cid:167) आपके मा(cid:197)यम से, माननीय मं(cid:253)ी जी से पूछना चाहता
ह(cid:243) ँ िक म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश म(cid:164) मेर े खरगौन-बड़वानी संसदीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) अनसु ूिचत जाित वग(cid:170) के काफ(cid:236) गांव ह(cid:167), उनम(cid:164)
पीएम-अजय योजना क(cid:236) (cid:179)या ि(cid:214)थित है? इस योजना के (cid:255)भावी ि(cid:248)या(cid:198)वयन और िनगरानी सिमित के
िलए (cid:179)या सरकार (cid:255)यास कर रही है?
सामािजक (cid:198)याय और अिधका(cid:229)रता म(cid:253)ं ी (डॉ. वीर(cid:198)े (cid:254) कुमार): माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, माननीय
सद(cid:214)य ने खरगौन लोक सभा (cid:177)े(cid:253) से संबंिधत (cid:255)(cid:307) पूछा है । खरगौन लोक सभा (cid:177)े(cid:253) अनुसूिचत जाित के
िलए आरि(cid:177)त (cid:174)ेणी के तहत आती है । वहा ँ पर अनसु ूिचत जाित के गांव(cid:332) क(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या कम है । खरगौन
लोक सभा (cid:177)े(cid:253) के अंतग(cid:170)त दो िजले- खरगौन और बड़वानी आते ह(cid:167) । खरगौन िजले म(cid:164) 30 गांव िचि(cid:313)त
िकये गये ह(cid:167), िजनम(cid:164) से 18 गांव(cid:332) को आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घोिषत िकया गया है । बड़वानी िजले म(cid:164) 7 गांव िचि(cid:313)त
िकये गये ह(cid:167) । वहा ँ िकसी भी गांव को आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम घोिषत नह(cid:233) िकया गया है । अनदु ान सहायता घटक
रा(cid:186)यवार जारी क(cid:236) जाती है । छा(cid:253)ावास घटक के अंतग(cid:170)त म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश के खरगौन संसदीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) से कोई
(cid:255)(cid:214)ताव नह(cid:233) आया है । जब वहा ँ से इसके िलए (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव आएगा, तो िनि(cid:306)त (cid:322)प से उसके िलए (cid:214)वीकृित

दी जाएगी ।
माननीय सद(cid:214)य ने मॉिनट(cid:229)रंग करने क(cid:236) (cid:211)यव(cid:214)था के संबंध म(cid:164) भी पूछा है । म(cid:167) बताना चाह(cid:243)ंगा िक
उसके िलए (cid:250)ामीण (cid:214)तर पर, िजला (cid:214)तर पर, रा(cid:186)य (cid:214)तर पर (cid:248)मश: (cid:250)ाम अिभसरण सिमित, िजला
अिभसरण सिमित, रा(cid:186)य अिभसरण सिमित और के (cid:198)(cid:254)ीय सलाहकार सिमित बनी ह(cid:242)ई है, जो इसम (cid:164)
िनगरानी का काम करती है । इसके साथ-साथ हमार े अिधकारी भी समय-समय पर रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) म(cid:164) जाकर
योजनाओ ंके बार े म(cid:164) समी(cid:177)ा करते ह(cid:167) । के (cid:198)(cid:254)ीय (cid:214)तर पर, म (cid:167) भी इसके संबंध म(cid:164), संपूण(cid:170) रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) के मंि(cid:253)य(cid:332)
और सिचव(cid:332) के साथ बैठक करके योजनाओ ंक(cid:236) (cid:255)गित क(cid:236) जानकारी लेता ह(cid:243) ँ ।
(cid:174)ी िवनोद लखमशी चावड़ा : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, माननीय (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी नर(cid:198)े (cid:254) मोदी जी के नेतृ(cid:194)व
म(cid:164) के (cid:198)(cid:254) सरकार के मा(cid:197)यम से, अनसु ूिचत जाित (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) गांव(cid:332) के िवकास म(cid:164) (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी- अनसु ूिचत जाित
अ(cid:203)यदु य योजना एक मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170) भूिमका िनभा रही है । म(cid:167) आपके मा(cid:197)यम से, माननीय मं(cid:253)ी जी से जानना
चाहता ह(cid:243) ँ िक गजु रात रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164), (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी- अनसु ूिचत जाित अ(cid:203)यदु य योजना क(cid:236) (cid:179)या ि(cid:214)थित है? मेर े
संसदीय (cid:177)े(cid:253) क(cid:184)छ म(cid:164), पीएम-अजय योजना के तहत िकतने गांव िचि(cid:313)त िकये गये ह(cid:167), उनम(cid:164) से िकतने
गांव(cid:332) म(cid:164) इस योजना का लाभ िमल रहा है?
डॉ. वीर(cid:198)े (cid:254) कु मार : माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) महोदय, माननीय सद(cid:214)य न े गजु रात रा(cid:186)य, िवशेषकर क(cid:184)छ लोक
सभा (cid:177)े(cid:253) के संबंध म,(cid:164) पीएम-अजय योजना के बार े म (cid:164) जानकारी मांगी है । यह योजना (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी-
अनसु ूिचत जाित अ(cid:203)यदु य योजना के नाम से जानी जाती है । इसम(cid:164) तीन घटक- आदश(cid:170) (cid:250)ाम, अनदु ान
सहायता और छा(cid:253)ावास ह(cid:167) । इन तीन(cid:332) घटक(cid:332) के िलए धनरािश जारी क(cid:236) जाती है । (cid:250)ाम िवकास सिमितया ँ
आव(cid:212)यकताओ ंका आकलन करती ह(cid:167), (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव बनाती ह(cid:167) और वे िजला व रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) के अनमु ोदन के बाद
के (cid:198)(cid:254) म(cid:164) आते ह(cid:167) ।
महोदय, माननीय सद(cid:214)य ने गुजरात क(cid:236) बात कही है, तो गजु रात रा(cid:186)य क(cid:236) कुल जनस(cid:180)ं या
6,04,39,692 है । उसम(cid:164) से अनसु ूिचत जाित क(cid:236) जनसं(cid:180)या 40,74,447 है, जो कुल जनसं(cid:180)या का

6.74 (cid:255)ितशत है । हमारी जो के (cid:198) (cid:254) (cid:255)ायोिजत योजना है, इसम(cid:164) पीएम-अजय के तहत, गजु रात रा(cid:186)य को

AFRICAN SWINE FEVER
*425. @SHRI PRADAN BARUAH:
Will the Minister of FISHERIES, ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING be
pleased to State:
(a) whether recent outbreak of African Swine Fever (ASF) in the country has
affected pig farmers, particularly small-scale and backyard farmers in terms of
economic losses, if so, the details thereof;
(b) the steps taken by the Government to compensate the farmers in most affected
States like Assam; and
(c) the details of steps taken by the Government to stop the spread of the disease?
म(cid:194)(cid:214)यपालन, पशुपालन और डेयरी मं(cid:253)ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा पंचायती राज मं(cid:253)ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य
म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:255)ो. एस. पी. िसहं बघेल):
(क) से (ग): िववरण सभा पटल पर रख िदया गया है।
@ Since Shri Pradan Baruah was not present, hence Hon’ble Speaker asked the Hon’ble Minister to lay the reply
and allowed Shri Gaurav Gogoi and Shri Tapir Gao to ask the Supplementary Questions.

िववरण
(क) से (ग): जी हां, रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा (cid:255)दान क(cid:236) गई सूचना के अनुसार, अ(cid:256)(cid:236)क(cid:236) (cid:214)वाइन फ(cid:236)वर (ASF) के
कुछ (cid:255)कोप ह(cid:242)ये ह(cid:167)। एएसएफ (ASF) संबंधी रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय काय(cid:170) योजना के अनुसार, सं(cid:248)िमत (cid:177)े(cid:253) के एक
िकलोमीटर के भीतर सूअर(cid:332) को मार देने (StampingOut) तथा उनके चार े को न(cid:310) करने क(cid:236) सलाह दी
गई है। क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार, पशुधन (cid:214)वा(cid:214)(cid:195)य एवं रोग िनयं(cid:253)ण काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (LHDCP) के उप-घटक पश ु रोग(cid:332) के
िनयं(cid:253)ण के िलए रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) को सहायता (ASCAD) के तहत, सं(cid:248)िमत (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) सूअर(cid:332) को मारने और पश ु
आहार न(cid:310) करने के िलए पशु मािलक(cid:332) को मुआवजा देने के िलए रा(cid:186)य क(cid:236) मांग के अनसु ार 50:50 (क(cid:164) (cid:254):
रा(cid:186)य) साझाकरण आधार पर रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) को िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता (cid:255)दान करती है।
सरकार िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत तरीके से (cid:255)भािवत िकसान(cid:332) को मुआवजा देती है जो इस (cid:255)कार है:
i. (cid:255)भािवत िकसान(cid:332) को सूअर(cid:332) को मारने और पश ु आहार को न(cid:310) करन े के िलए िदया जाने वाला
मुआवजा इस (cid:255)कार है: 15 िकलो(cid:250)ाम तक के सूअर के ब(cid:184)च(cid:332) के िलए 2200 (cid:321)पये, 15-40
िकलो(cid:250)ाम तक के (cid:250)ोअर/वय(cid:214)क के िलए 5800 (cid:321)पये, 40-70 िकलो(cid:250)ाम तक के (cid:255)जनन करने

100 िकलो(cid:250)ाम से अिधक के िलए 15000 (cid:321)पये। इसके अलावा, पशु आहार के िलए 22.00
(cid:321)पये (cid:255)ित िकलो(cid:250)ाम क(cid:236) दर स े मुआवजा िदया जाता है। रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) से (cid:255)ा(cid:302) मांग के अनसु ार

है।

IMPACT OF TARIFF REDUCTIONS

*426. DR. NAMDEO KIRSAN

(a) whether the Government is aware of the recent statement of NITI Aayog that

economic growth, if so, the details thereof;

three years, sector-wise;
(c) the details of tariff increases implemented by the Government during the said
period sector-wise; and;
(d) the details of impact of these tariff reductions on domestic manufacturing and
international trade competitiveness?

01.04.2025 91
15TH FINANCE COMMISSION GRANTS
*429. SHRI A. RAJA:
Will the Minister of PANCHAYATI RAJ be pleased to state:
(a) whether 15th Finance Commission has recommended funds to Gram
Panchayats in the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) the total funds provided to the funds to the Local Self Governments (LSGs) by
the Union Government and the State Governments, in the country, State-wise,
particularly to Tamil Nadu;
(d) whether there is any plan to provide additional funds or grants to LSGs due to
loss of revenue to local bodies due to GST and other cess;
(e) if so, the details thereof; and
(f) if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF PANCHAYATI RAJ; AND MINISTER OF FISHERIES,
ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING (SHRI RAJIV RANJAN SINGH ALIAS
LALAN SINGH):
(a) and (b): Yes, Sir. Fifteenth Finance Commission (XV FC) has recommended
grants to the tune of Rs. 60,750 crores for the interim period FY 2020-21 and
Rs.2,36,805 crore for the period FY 2021-26 to Panchayats in all the three tiers and
Traditional Local Bodies and Sixth Schedule areas in 28 States.
01.04.2025 92
The criteria for fund allocation determined by the Fifteenth Finance
Commission involve inter-se distribution among the States, with a weightage of 90
percent assigned to population and 10 percent to the areas of the States.
Fifteenth Finance Commission recommended grants are released to the
State Governments by Union Government for onwards transfer to the
panchayats/traditional bodies within the 10 working days. Any delay beyond 10
working days the State Government is required to release the grant with interest for
the period of delay as per the average effective rate of interest on market
borrowings/State Development Loans for the previous year.
The inter-se distribution among all the tiers to be done by the State
Governments on the basis of the accepted recommendations of the latest State
Finance Commission (SFC) and in conformity with the following bands;
Range for Gram Block District
Distribution Panchayats Panchayats Panchayats
Minimum 70% 10% 5%
Maximum 85% 25% 15%
States which have a two-tier system with only village and district panchayats,
the allocation shall be in the followings bands:
Range for Gram District
Distribution Panchayats Panchayats
Minimum 70% 15%
Maximum 85% 30%
01.04.2025 93
The State-wise and Year-wise allocation of Fifteenth Finance Commission
(XV FC) Grants to Gram Panchayats/equivalent is given in the enclosed
Statement-I.
(c): The Central Finance Commission grants to Rural Local Bodies are continuously
increasing from 13th Finance Commission to 14th Finance Commission and further
from 14th Finance Commission to 15th Finance Commission. The allocations under
13th Finance Commission (FY 2010-15) were Rs. 64,408 Crore and under 14th
Finance Commission (FY 2015-2020) were Rs. 2,00,292.20 Crore. The allocation
under 15th Finance Commission (FY 2021-26) is Rs. 2,36,805 Crore which is
around 4 times of 13th Finance Commission allocation.
State-wise allocation and release of funds to the Rural Local Bodies under
the Fifteenth Finance Commission (XV FC) grants are given in the enclosed
Statement-II.
The total fund of Rs. 13303.50 crore and Rs. 28021.21 crore have been
provided to the Rural Local Self Governments (RLSGs) in Tamil Nadu by the Union
Government under the Fifteenth Finance Commission & the State Government
under the State Finance Commission, respectively. The details of the total funds
provided to the Rural Local Self Governments (RLSGs) by the Union Government
and the Tamil Nadu Government are given below:
01.04.2025 94

(d) to (f): The Ministry of Finance releases grants to the States for the Rural Local
Self Government (RLSG) under the Finance Commission Awards. Article 280 of
the Constitution of India provides the basis for the Central Finance Commissions to
assess the status of finances of the Union, States and their respective local bodies
and recommend sharing of taxes as well as grants for various purposes to the
States and Local Bodies, the sharing of taxes includes GST & excludes Cess. The
Quantum of grant to be allocated to Local Self Governments and the factors to be
considered for it are the prerogative of the Finance Commission.

PROMOTION OF ORGANIC FARMING
*431. SHRI ANUP SANJAY DHOTRE:
SHRI SANDIPANRAO ASARAM BHUMARE:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE be pleased to
state:
(a) the present status and details of initiatives taken by the Government to promote
organic farming in the country, State-wise particularly in Maharashtra, Madhya
Pradesh and Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli;
01.04.2025 101
(b) whether any steps have been taken or proposed to be taken by the Government
to make the pattern of this traditional farming economical and profitable, if so,
the details thereof;
(c) the details of the funds earmarked/allocated, released and utilised for such
initiatives particularly for the said States/UTs;
(d) whether any training workshops and awareness programmes are being
organized by the Government to create awareness about techniques and
benefits of organic farming in the country particularly in the said States/UTs and
if so, the details thereof; and
(e) the number of such workshops organised by the Government in the country
during the last three years and the current year along with the number of farmers
participated in these workshops including Maharashtra?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) and (b): Government is promoting organic farming through the schemes of
Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) in all the States/UTs and Mission
Organic Value Chain Development for North Eastern Region (MOVCDNER)
scheme is being implemented. Both the schemes stress on end-to-end support to
farmers engaged in organic farming i.e. from production to post-harvest
management training and capacity building. The main focus of the PKVY and
MOVCDNER schemes is to promote natural resource based integrated and climate
01.04.2025 102
resilient sustainable farming systems that ensure maintenance and increase of soil
fertility, natural resource conservation, on-farm nutrient recycling and minimize
dependence of farmers on external inputs.
So far, 59.74 lakh ha area has been covered under organic farming since
2015-16 including Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh and Union Territory of Dadra &
Nagar Haveli. The State wise details of area covered under organic farming
National Programme for Organic Production (NPOP) (including MOVCDNER) +
Participatory Guarantee System (PGS) under PKVY till 2023-2024 is given in the
enclosed Statement.
PKVY is being implemented in a cluster mode, each cluster with farmer
groups having area of 20 ha. Under PKVY, assistance of Rs.31,500/ha over 3 years
is provided for promotion of organic farming to cover different components like
training, PGS Certification, value addition, marketing and publicity. Out of this,
assistance of Rs.15,000 per ha over three years is provided to farmers through
DBT for on- farm /off –farm organic inputs.
Under MOVCDNER scheme, assistance of Rs.46,500/ha over 3 years is
provided for creation of FPOs, support to farmers for organic inputs, quality seeds/
planting material and training, hand holding and certification. Out of this, the
assistance of Rs.15,000 per ha over 3 years is given as DBT is provided to farmers
for on- farm /off –farm organic inputs and Rs.17,500 for the planting material.
01.04.2025 103
Through financial assistance, hand holding support and training for
production of eco-friendly fertilizers from locally available items, the cost of input
reduces and farming become economical and profitable.
(c): The details of funds allocated released & utilized for promoting organic farming
under PKVY scheme in Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh & Union Territory of Dadar&
Nagar Havelisince 2015-16 to 2024-25 (as on 26.03.2025) is as below:
(Rs. in Crore)
State Allocation Released Utilized
Maharashtra 226.52 133.13 116.43
Madhya Pradesh 456.15 146.64 125.11
Dadar& Nagar Haveli 75.50 10.00 0.00
(d) and (e): PKVY & MOVCDNER schemes is implemented through the States/UTs.
The details of training, workshops and awareness programmeare maintained at the
State/UTs level and not centrally.
Government of Maharashtra has informed that 2786 trainings have been conducted
in the same duration covering 1,54,162 farmers.
Government of Union Territory of Dadar& Nagar Haveli has informed that 7
workshops have been conducted in the same durationcovering 232 farmers.
Government of Madhya Pradesh has informed that 7 workshopshave been
conducted in the same duration covering 100 farmers.

(ङ) ित(cid:321)नेलवेली िजले के अंतग(cid:170)त उ(cid:265) योजना के तहत शािमल (cid:214)थल(cid:332) का (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;

(छ) बुिनयादी ढांचा संबंधी प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ं क(cid:236) िनरतं र आयोजना और समयब(cid:294) समि(cid:198)वत िन(cid:213)पादन के

वािण(cid:186)य और उ(cid:299)ोग म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी पीयूष गोयल):
(क): पीएम गितशि(cid:265) रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय मा(cid:214) टर (cid:200) लान (पीएमजीएस एनएमपी) क(cid:236) श(cid:321)ु आत 13 अ(cid:179) टूबर 2021 को

DAIRY COOPERATIVES IN TELANGANA
* 433. SHRI VAMSI KRISHNA GADDAM:
Will the Minister of FISHERIES, ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING be
pleased to state:
(a) the details of funds allocated and the percentage utilised by dairy cooperatives
in Telangana during the last three years;
(b) whether the Government has assessed reasons for the decline in cooperative
milk production in districts like Nizamabad and Warangal;
(c) the steps taken to revive ailing cooperatives; and
(d) whether any request has been received from the State of Telangana for
increasing central funds under cooperative development schemes, if so, the
steps taken in this regard?
01.04.2025 117
THE MINISTER OF PANCHAYATI RAJ; AND MINISTER OF FISHERIES,
ANIMAL HUSBANDRY AND DAIRYING (SHRI RAJIV RANJAN SINGH ALIAS
LALAN SINGH):
(a) : The details of funds released by the Department of Animal Husbandry and
Dairying (DAHD), Government of India (GoI) under dairy development Schemesto
Dairy Cooperatives in Telangana during the last three years (2021-22, 22-23 and
23-24) and the percentage utilized is as under:

01.04.2025 118
(b) : The milk production in the Telangana increased to 5840 thousand tonnes
in 2023-24 from 4207 thousand tonnes in 2014-15 with significant increase of
38.81 % in milk production over the past decade. However, the Government of
Telangana has recognized and assessed the several factors that have contributed
for decline in cooperative milk production in districts like Nizamabad and
Warangal. Some of the factors are as under:
1. Inadequate green fodder facilities
2. Increased feeding costs- High costs of Feed ingredients and Concentrates
3. Low productivity from milch animals due to inadequate green fodder facilities
In order to strengthen fodder seed production chain to improve availability of
certified fodder seed required for fodder production and encouraging
entrepreneurs for establishment of fodder Block/Hay Bailing/Silage Making Units
through incentivization, DAHD, GoI is implementing sub-Mission for feed and
fodder development under National Livestock Mission(NLM). An amount of Rs 592
lakh has been released to Telangana for quality fodder seed production.
(c) : DAHD, GoI is implementing the following Dairy Development schemes across
the country to supplement the efforts made by the State Government
forstrengthening/upgradation of milk processing infrastructure of
DairyCooperatives:
(i) National Programme for Dairy Development (NPDD): NPDD is
implemented with following 2 components:
01.04.2025 119
a. Component 'A' of NPDD focuses on creating and strengthening
infrastructure for quality milk testing and primary chilling facilities. It benefits
State Cooperative Dairy Federations, District Cooperative Milk Producers’
Unions, Self Help Groups (SHGs), Milk Producer Companies, and Farmer
Producer Organizations. This component aims to improve milk quality and
safety from production to storage.
b. Component 'B' of NPDD ("Dairying through Cooperatives") enhances the
sale of milk and milk products by increasing farmers' access to organized
market. It upgrades milk processing and marketing infrastructure, boosting
the capacity of producer-owned institutions. This component ensures better
market linkage and processing efficiency.
(ii) Supporting Dairy Cooperatives & Farmer Producer Organisations
engaged in dairy activities (SDCFPO): This initiative aims to aid State
Dairy Cooperative Federations by offering soft working capital loans to help
them navigate crises caused by severely adverse market conditions or
natural calamities.
(iii) Animal Husbandry Infrastructure Development Fund (AHIDF): The
primary objectives alsoinclude enhancing milk processing capacity and
encouraging product diversification. These measures aim to provide better
access for unorganized rural milk producers to organized market, thereby
fostering growth and inclusivity in the sector.
01.04.2025 120
In addition to above, with support of Government of India the Governmentof
Telanganahas initiated several measures to revive dairycooperatives like Vijaya
Dairy, which includes:
i. Incentive Payments to Dairy Farmers: The Telangana Government
introduced Rs.4 per litre incentive to dairy farmers to offset production cost.
ii. Increased milk procurement prices to support small and marginal farmers.
iii. Subsidized feed concentrates programs to reduce feeding cost and
improve livestock health.
iv. Training programs for dairy farmers to enhance management skills and
increase productivity.
v. Financial support through loan (e.g., Mudra, PMEGP (Prime Minister's
Employment Generation Programme), Stree Nidhi, and Kisan Credit Cards
(KCC)) for cattle purchase and working capital.
vi. Interest subvention on working capital loan to ease financial pressure on
dairycooperatives.
vii. Artificial Insemination (AI) services provided to farmers to improve cattle
genetics and milk yields.
Further, the Government of Telangana informed that support from the Ministry of
Rural Development (MoRD), Under Shyama Prasad Mukharjee RURBAN Scheme
An amount of Rs.12.30 Cr was allotted under the RURBAN scheme by the District
Collectors of Sangareddy, Rangareddy, Warangal, Nagrkurnool, Mahbubnagar,
01.04.2025 121
Medak, Jangaon and Kamareddy Districts of Telangana towards establishment of
Milk chilling and processing infrastructure.
(d) : No Sir, there is no request from Telangana for increased Central funds.
However, the schemesare demand-driven, and projects submitted by the State
Governmentare considered as per the norms of the scheme guidelines. The
details of project sanctioned under dairy development schemes for
Cooperatives in Telangana is as under:
i. NPDD Scheme:
a. Under Component A of NPDD Scheme, 8 projects have been
sanctioned in Telangana with total project cost of Rs. 8916.24 lakh,
out of which Central share is Rs. 6967.31 lakh and out of this, an
amount of Rs. 3770.88 lakh has been released and Rs. 3120.25 lakh
has been utilized so far.
b. Under Component B of the NPDD Scheme, one project has been
sanctioned in Telangana with a total project cost of Rs. 9070.94 lakh,
comprising a loan of Rs. 7152.61 lakh, a grant of Rs. 1246.30 lakh,
and a producer institution contribution of Rs. 672.03 lakh. So far, Rs.
4522.19 lakh has been released, which includes a loan amount of Rs.
4161.71 lakh and a grant amount of Rs. 360.48 lakh to support the
project’s implementation.
01.04.2025 122
ii. SDCFPO: As on 16.03.2025, Rs 65.15 lakh (Rs 39 lakh regular interest
subsidy and Rs 26.15 lakh additional interest subsidy amount) has been
released against the working capital loan amount of Rs 150 crore @ 2% per
annum to 2 Milk Unions/Federations.
iii. AHIDF: 3 proposals have been approved under the erstwhileDairy
Processing and Infrastructure Development Fund (DIDF)Scheme with a total
cost of Rs. 261 crore including loan of Rs. 156.70 crore, out of which Rs
134.22 Cr has been released under the scheme on which interest subvention
has been provided.
KISAN CREDIT CARD LOANS
*434. SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA:
Will the Minister of AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government is aware that with introduction of Kisan Credit Card
bad loans have increased 42% in last four years due to unremunerative
agricultural prices and MSP for crops;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government is likely to implement the recommendations of
Swaminathan Commission regarding MSP to distress the agriculture sector;
(d) if so, the details thereof; and
01.04.2025 123
(e) if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) and (b): There has been consistent decline in share of Non Performing Assets
(NPAs) in Kisan Credit Card (KCC) accounts across various banking sectors in last
few yearsas detailed in the following table:
Status of NPA (%) – KCC Scheme
KCC - NPA (%) as on
Bank Group 31st Mar 2022 31st Mar 2023 31st Mar 2024 31st Dec
2024
SCBs 15.1 14.9 14.0 14.16
RRBs 9.5 7.8 7.1 NA*
Coop 6.9 6.8 6.5 NA*
*Not Available Data Source: RBI and NABARD
Though the absolute NPA amount in KCC accounts has shown increase over
the years, there has also been a commensurate increase in the overall agriculture
credit extended under KCC accounts over these years. During this period, the credit
extended to the KCC accounts increased significantly from Rs 9.38 lakh crore as
on 31 March 2022 to Rs 10.05 lakhs crore as on 31 Dec 2024.
(c) and (d): The National Commission on Farmers (NCF) was constituted under
the Chairmanship of Prof. M. S. Swaminathan in 2004. Based on the 'Draft
National Policy for Farmers', submitted by the Swaminathan Commission, which
contained major recommendations of the Commission, the Government approved
01.04.2025 124
the 'National Policy for Farmers in 2007' (NPF 2007), which aimed to improve
economic viability of farming and increase net income of farmers. In relation to
these recommendations, an Inter- Ministerial Committee (IMC) was constituted in
the year 2007, which recommended 201 Action Points to be implemented by the
Government. All 201 Action Points have been implemented.
National Commission on Farmers (NCF) also recommended that Minimum
Support Price (MSP) should be at least 50 percent more than the weighted
average cost of production. To give effect to this recommendation, Government,
in its Union Budget for 2018-19, had made an announcement to keep MSP at a
level of minimum one and half times of the cost of production as a pre-determined
principle. Accordingly, MSP for all mandated Kharif, Rabi and other commercial
crops have been fixed with a margin of at least 50 per cent over weighted average
cost of production.
(e): Does not arise.
RECYCLING OF TEXTILE WASTE
*435. MD. RAKIBUL HUSSAIN:
Will the Minister of TEXTILES be pleased to state :
(a) the measures taken by the Government in light of increasing environmental
concerns about textile waste and water pollution;
01.04.2025 125
(b) whether the Government has any plans to promote the adoption of modern,
environment-friendly technologies in textile manufacturing and if so, the details
thereof; and
(c) whether the Government has any proposal/plan to support the recycling and
reuse of textile waste, particularly in Assam's handloom and small-scale textile
sectors, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF TEXTILES (SHRI GIRIRAJ SINGH):
(a): In order to facilitate the textile industry to meet the required environmental
standards and to support new Common Effluent Treatment Plants (CETP)/
upgradation of CETPs in processing clusters/ processing parks, the Government is
implementing Integrated Processing Development Scheme (IPDS) since 2013. The
scheme aims to facilitate the Indian textile industry to become globally competitive
using environmentally friendly processing standards and technology. The scheme
would also be applicable for technology upgradation and capacity enhancement in
existing Textile Clusters. The scheme was in implementation upto 31.03.2021.
Now, the scheme is being implemented for completing ongoing projects only.
Further, in order to mitigate effects of increasing textile waste, Government has
undertaken multiple activities including a study on Mapping of Textile Waste Value
Chain, an MoU among Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation, Textile Committee and
others to develop a centre for processing of textile waste in Navi Mumbai, an MoU
among Textile Committee, Standing Conference of Public Enterprises (SCOPE)
01.04.2025 126
and the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) to promote the use of upcycled products
in Government procurement.
(b): With a view to incentivize the modernisation of textile machinery including
environmentally friendly technologies, Government is implementing Amended
Technology Upgradation Fund Scheme (ATUFS), which was valid up to
31.03.2022. The scheme is currently ongoing for meeting committed liabilities only.
(c): There is no specific proposal from Assam’s handloom and small scale textile
sector under consideration for recycling and reuse of textile waste.
NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF SOCIAL DEFENCE
*436. DR. RICKY A. J. SYNGKON:
Will the Minister of SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether Government proposes to establish a National Institute of Social
Defence (NISD) in Shillong Parliamentary Constituency, Meghalaya to address
critical societal challenges such as the country’s highest percentage of female-
headed households (41% as per NFHS), the alarming rise in drug addiction and
the prevalence of early marriage etc.;
(b) if so, the details thereof and if not, the alternate steps taken by the Government
to tackle these pressing issues in Meghalaya, particularly those impacting
women, youth and families;
01.04.2025 127
(c) whether the Ministry plans to formulate a region-specific action plan under the
proposed institute to provide tailored programmes for women’s empowerment,
youth rehabilitation and social welfare in Meghalaya; and
(d) whether in view of growing drug addiction crisis in the region, the Ministry
proposes to prioritize collaboration with local NGOs, community leaders and
educational institutions to implement preventive and rehabilitative strategies
effectively and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT (DR. VIRENDRA
KUMAR):
(a) to (c): There is no such proposal under consideration for setting up National
Institute of Social Defence (NISD) in Meghalaya. Steps taken by the Department of
Social Justice & Empowerment (DoSJE) for drug demand reduction in the country
including Meghalaya is given in the enclosed Statement.
(d): DoSJE is implementing National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction
(NAPDDR), a centrally sponsored scheme under which financial assistance is
provided to States for Preventive Education and Awareness Generation, Capacity
Building, Programmes for Drug Demand Reduction and NGOs/VOs for running and
maintenance of Integrated Rehabilitation Centres for Addicts (lRCAs), Community
based peer Led intervention (CPLI) for early Drug Use Prevention among
Adolescents, Outreach and Drop In Centres (ODIC) and District De-Addiction
Centres (DDACs).
01.04.2025 128
NashaMukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA) is being implemented in all districts of
the country including all the districts of Meghalaya. NMBA has reached out to the
community and spread awareness about substance use with focus on higher
educational Institutions, university campuses, schools, students, youth & women.
Under the State Action Plan, various activities such as Preventive Education and
Awareness Generation, Capacity Building, treatment and rehabilitation, research
etc. are taken up with the involvement of various stakeholders such as districts,
educational and other institutes, community, students, youth and women etc.
STATEMENT
The Department of Social Justice & Empowerment is the nodal Department for drug
demand reduction in the country including Meghalaya. To tackle the issue of
substance use, the Department is implementing National Action Plan for Drug
Demand Reduction (NAPDDR), a centrally sponsored scheme under which
financial assistance is provided to:
i. State Governments/ Union Territory (UT) Administrations for Preventive
Education and Awareness Generation, Capacity Building, Programmes for Drug
Demand Reduction by States/UTs etc.
ii. NGOs/VOs for running and maintenance of Integrated Rehabilitation Centres for
Addicts (lRCAs), Community based peer Led intervention (CPLI) for early Drug Use
Prevention among Adolescents, Outreach and Drop In Centres (ODIC) and District
De-Addiction Centres (DDACs); and
01.04.2025 129
iii. Government Hospitals for Addiction Treatment Facilities (ATFs).
2. The following activities have been undertaken under NAPDDR scheme:
i. Currently 350 IRCAs, 46 CPLIs, 74 ODICs, 124 DDACs and 142 ATFs in
Government Hospitals are being financially supported by the Department of Social
Justice & Empowerment. All of these facilities have been geo-tagged for ease of
access to those in need. Out of these, 01 IRCA, 01 ODIC, 01 DDAC and 01 ATF
are located in Meghalaya.
ii. State specific State Action Plan (SAP) is formulated by the State for undertaking
various drug demand reduction activities in the region and funds are released by
the Department under SAP under the scheme of NAPDDR.
iii. A Toll-free Helpline for de-addiction, '14446' is being maintained by the Ministry
of Social Justice & Empowerment for providing primary counseling and immediate
referral services to the persons seeking help through this helpline. More than 4.18
lakh calls have been received so far on the helpline number. Out of these, more
than 11 thousand calls have been received from Meghalaya.
iv. NashaMukt Bharat Abhiyaan (NMBA) was launched on 15th August 2020 by
Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment in 272 identified districts and now it has
been extended to all districts across the country. NashaMukt Bharat Abhiyaan
intends to reach out to the masses and spread awareness about substance use
with focus on higher educational Institutions, university campuses, schools,
students, youth & women.
01.04.2025 130
v. Till now, through the various activities undertaken on-ground under NMBA,
15.44+ crore people have been sensitized on substance use including 5.17+ crore
Youth and 3.27+ crore Women. Participation of 4.18+ lakh educational institutions
has ensured that the message of the Abhiyaan reaches children and youth of the
country. In Meghalaya, more than 15.32 lakh people have been sensitized including
more than 28 thousand youth, 12+ thousand women and with participation of more
than 2 thousand educational institutions.
vi. MoUs have been signed with Spiritual organizations like The Art of Living,
Brahma Kumaris, SantNirankari Mission, ISKCON, Shri Ram Chandra Mission and
All World GayatriPariwar to support NMBA and conduct mass awareness activities.
vii. Awareness is also being spread through official Social Media accounts of the
Abhiyaan on Twitter, Facebook & Instagram.
viii. NMBA Website (http://nmba.dosje.gov.in) provides detailed information to the
user/viewer about the Abhiyaan, an online discussion forum, NMBA dashboard, e-
pledge.
ix. A Mass Pledge/ Oath on NMBA was conducted on 12th August, 2024 and online
across the country and a total of about 3+ crore people from 2+ lakh institutions
participated in the nationwide pledge.
x. The Ministry has developed Navchetna Modules (A New Consciousness on Life
Skills and Drug Education for School Children) - teacher training modules. The
Navchetna module aim to increase awareness and education on life skills and drugs
01.04.2025 131
among students in schools in India. In the state of Meghalaya, in first phase of
NAVCHETNA module, 13 Master Trainers have been trained from 04 districts for
providing training to Teachers in second phase of the module.
xi. Ministry through its autonomous body National Institute of Social Defence (NISD)
and other collaborating agencies like SCERTs, KendriyaVidyalayaSangathan etc.
provides for regular awareness generation and sensitization sessions for all
stakeholders including students, teachers, parents etc. During the current year (FY
2024- 25), 05 programs have been conducted by NISD for various target groups in
State of Meghalaya. 2298 people have participated in these programs.
PMAY-G FUNDS FOR WEST BENGAL
*437. SHRI KALIPADA SAREN KHERWAL:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether the funds of Pradhan MantriAwasYojana-Gramin (PMAY-G) for West
Bengal are pending with the Government; and
(b) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) and (b): Under the Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G), overall
the State of West Bengal has been allocated a target of 45,69,423 houses out of
which the State has sanctioned all houses, released 1st instalment of assistance to
01.04.2025 132
34,65,970 beneficiaries and 34,19,213 houses have been completed and
11,50,210 houses are yet to be completed, as of 26.03.2025.
During Financial Year (FY) 2021-22, the Ministry of Rural Development had
received complaints from the Members of Parliament, Members of State Legislative
Assembly (West Bengal) and other stakeholders with serious allegations of re-
naming of the scheme name as Bangla AwaasYojana in the State and also grant
of assistance to ineligible households. These complaints were verified through
inspection by the National Level Monitoring (NLM) teams. The complaints were
found to be true in most of the cases. Thereafter, the State Government submitted
compliances on the observations of the NLM teams and complaints of the Members
of Parliament and Members of Legislative Assembly. The Action Taken Report
(ATR) submitted by the State Government during August, 2022 was accepted. The
State Government had also agreed that since the ‘Name’ and ‘Logo’ are part of the
core features of the Centrally Sponsored Schemes (CSSs), the official ‘Name’ and
‘Logo’ of PMAY-G will be used and displayed in the Citizen and Public Information
Board regularly. The State Government also issued instructions to the District
Magistrates for proper implementation of the PMAY-G with official name and 'logo'
of the scheme.
The compliances submitted by the State Government were accepted and
target of 11,36,488 houses from finalised Awaas+ 2018 survey list was allocated to
the State during the FY 2022-23 vide D.O. letter dated 24.11.2022.
01.04.2025 133
The Ministry has already released Rs 25,798 crore as Central share to the
State since 2016-17 till 2021-22 for implementation of the PMAY-G.
However, post allocation of target to the state in November, 2022, complaints
regarding irregularities in implementation of PMAY-G were received. These
complaints were verified through Senior Officers Team and National Level
Monitoring (NLM) Teams. No Central share of funds have been released to the
State of West Bengal under the PMAY-G with the approval of the competent
authority in the Ministry during FY 2022-23, 2023-24 and 2024-25 (till date) for
targets of FY 2022-23 from the finalized Awaas+ 2018 survey lists due to non
submission of satisfactory Action Taken Reports (ATR) on the observations of the
NLM teams and Senior Officers’ Teams of the Ministry of Rural Development. The
ATRs have been received from the state and is under examination.
The balance liability of Central share under PMAY-G for the State of West
Bengal till 31.03.2024 to be released during FY 2024-25 is Rs. 7,888.67 crore.
BENEFICIARIES UNDER MGNREGS
*438. ADV. DEAN KURIAKOSE:
Will the Minister of RURAL DEVELOPMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether labourers under Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment
Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS) have been declining in the State of Kerala;
(b) if so, the details thereof, district-wise;
01.04.2025 134
(c) the total number of beneficiaries of Kerala under MGNREGS during the last five
years, district-wise;
(d) whether there is a demand from the dairy sector to include labourers engaged
under the cattle fodder production as well as the related dairy sector to be
included under MGNREGS; and
(e) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FARMERS WELFARE; AND
MINISTER OF RURAL DEVELOPMENT (SHRI SHIVRAJ SINGH CHOUHAN):
(a) and (b): Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme
(Mahatma Gandhi NREGS) is a demand driven wage employment Scheme which
provides for the enhancement of livelihood security of the households in rural areas
of the country by providing at least one hundred days of guaranteed wage
employment in every financial year to every household whose adult members
volunteer to do unskilled manual work. It is a fall back option which provide
livelihood security to the rural households, when no better employment opportunity
is available. Details of year-wise registered workers in Kerala under Mahatma
Gandhi NREGS during the last three financial years and the current financial year
2024-25 (as on 26.03.2025) is given below:
01.04.2025 135
Year-wise registered workers in Kerala under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS
during the last three financial years and current financial year 2024-25 (as on
26.03.2025)
Number of registered
workers since inception (in Workers registered during
Financial year lakh) the financial year (in lakh)
2021-22 63.57 3.47
2022-23 62.52 1.81
2023-24 58.28 1.50
2024-25
57.24 1.53
(as on 26.03.25)
(As per NREGASoft)
District-wise details of registered workers in Kerala under Mahatma Gandhi
NREGS during the last three financial years and the current financial year 2024-25
(as on 26.03.2025) is given in the enclosed Statement-I.
(c): District-wise number of workers who availed employment in Kerala under
Mahatma Gandhi NREGS during the last five financial years from 2019-20 to 2023-
24 are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(d) and (e): As per Section 29 of the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment
Guarantee Act (Mahatma Gandhi NREGA), Central Government may add new
works to the permissible list of works under Schedule 1 of the Act. The revision of
permissible works is a regular exercise under the Scheme. As and when any
request for inclusion of any work in the list of permissible activities under Mahatma
Gandhi NREGS are received from State Government or need for the same is felt
01.04.2025 136
by the Central Government, the matter is examined on merit and a decision is
taken.
Under the Scheme, several activities related to the dairy sector, including the
creation of infrastructure for livestock promotion, such as poultry shelters, goat
shelters, piggery shelters, cattle shelters, and fodder troughs for cattle are already
covered. Additionally, the development of silvipasture grasslands for fodder on
individual and community lands is an allowable activity under Mahatma Gandhi
NREGS. These provisions facilitate the construction of assets that support livestock
rearing and fodder production, thereby benefiting the dairy sector.
Here, it is mentioned that as per para 4(3) of Schedule-I of Mahatma Gandhi
National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, "works which are non-tangible, not
measurable, repetitive in nature such as, removing grass, pebbles, agricultural
operations, shall not be taken up under the scheme".

और उनके संबंिधत (cid:214)थानीय िनकाय(cid:332) के िव(cid:176) क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित का आकलन करन े और रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) और (cid:214)थानीय
िनकाय(cid:332) को िविभ(cid:198)न उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य(cid:332) के िलए कर(cid:332) के साथ-साथ अनदु ान(cid:332) के बंटवार े क(cid:236) िसफा(cid:229)रश करन े का

आधार (cid:255)दान करता है। िव(cid:176) आयोग के अनदु ान का उपयोग (cid:250)ामीण (cid:214)थानीय िनकाय(cid:332) यानी पंचायत(cid:332)
(cid:300)ारा वेतन या अ(cid:198)य (cid:214)थापना (cid:211)यय को छोड़कर संिवधान क(cid:236) (cid:181)यारहव(cid:233) अनसु ूची म (cid:164) िनिहत 29 िवषय(cid:332) के
तहत (cid:214)थान-िविश(cid:310) महसूस क(cid:236) जाने वाली आव(cid:212)यकताओ ंके िलए िकया जा सकता है।
पं(cid:254)हव(cid:164) िव(cid:176) आयोग के तहत, अंत(cid:229)रम अविध िव(cid:176) वष(cid:170) 2020-2021 के िलए 60,750 करोड़

करोड़ (cid:321)पये 28 रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) म(cid:164) तीन(cid:332) (cid:214)तर(cid:332) और पारपं (cid:229)रक (cid:214)थानीय िनकाय(cid:332) और छठी अनसु ूची (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164)
पंचायत(cid:332) को आवंिटत िकए गए ह(cid:167)। पं(cid:254)हव (cid:164) िव(cid:176) आयोग अनदु ान के दो घटक ह(cid:167)- ब(cid:294) (टाइड) और अब(cid:294)
(अनटाइड)।
पं(cid:254)हव(cid:164) िव(cid:176) आयोग के तहत कुल अनदु ान का 40% अब(cid:294) (अनटाइड) अनदु ान, वेतन या अ(cid:198)य
(cid:214)थापना (cid:211)यय को छोड़कर, संिवधान क(cid:236) (cid:181)यारहव(cid:233) अनसु ूची म(cid:164) िनिहत 29 िवषय(cid:332) के तहत (cid:214)थान-िविश(cid:310)
महसूस क(cid:236) जाने वाली आव(cid:212)यकताओ ंके िलए (cid:250)ामीण (cid:214)थानीय िनकाय(cid:332)(cid:300)ारा उपयोग िकया जा सकता
है।
ब(cid:294) (टाइड) अनदु ान, जो िक कुल अनुदान का 60% है, बुिनयादी सेवाओ ंजैसे (क) (cid:214)व(cid:184)छता

के िलए इ(cid:214)तेमाल िकया जा सकता है । यिद िकसी (cid:214)थानीय िनकाय न े एक (cid:174)ेणी क(cid:236) आव(cid:212)यकताओ ंको

(ख) और (ग): क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय िव(cid:176) आयोग अनदु ान से पंचायत(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा िकए गए (cid:211)यय क(cid:236) समी(cid:177)ा करन े के िलए,

लॉ(cid:198)च िकया है। ई-(cid:250)ाम(cid:214)वराज पंचायत के योजना से लेकर भगु तान तक के सभी िव(cid:176)ीय काय(cid:334) को
एक(cid:236)कृत करता है। इस (cid:255)गित को डैशबोड(cid:170) पर एक(cid:253) िकया जाता है।

िवकास योजनाए,ं िव(cid:176), िनवा(cid:170)िचत (cid:255)ितिनिध और संक(cid:208)प शािमल ह(cid:167)। (cid:250)ाम पंचायत म(cid:164) ई-गवन(cid:166)स के मा(cid:197)यम
से नाग(cid:229)रक क(cid:164) ि(cid:254)त योजना को बढ़ाने के िलए (cid:250)ाम सभा क(cid:236) बैठक(cid:332) का समय िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत करन,े नाग(cid:229)रक(cid:332)
को सूिचत करन,े एज(cid:164)डा (cid:255)सा(cid:229)रत करन,े पंचायत के िनण(cid:170)य(cid:332) को (cid:229)रकॉड(cid:170) करन े आिद के िलए पंचायत
िनण(cid:170)य ए(cid:200)लीके शन भी िवकिसत िकया गया है।
इसके अलावा, पंचायत खात(cid:332) यानी (cid:250)ाम पंचायत(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:255)ाि(cid:302)य(cid:332) और (cid:211)यय(cid:332) का समय पर ऑिडट सिु नि(cid:306)त

( https://auditonline.gov.in ) श(cid:322)ु िकया है। यह एि(cid:200)लके शन न के वल पंचायत खात(cid:332) क(cid:236) ऑिडिटंग
क(cid:236) सिु वधा (cid:255)दान करता है, बि(cid:208)क लेखापरी(cid:177)ा (cid:229)रकॉड(cid:170) बनाए रखन े क(cid:236) सिु वधा भी (cid:255)दान करता है। यह

GLACIAL LAKE OUTBURST FLOOD MITIGATION
*440. SHRI BALABHADRA MAJHI:
SHRI TRIVENDRA SINGH RAWAT:
Will the Minister of HOME AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the steps being taken to strengthen early warning systems, particularly in remote
and inaccessible areas;
(b) the details of plans of the Government to involve local communities in monitoring
and alerting mechanisms to ensure last-mile connectivity;
01.04.2025 147
(c) the manner in which the Government plans to assess and minimize the
economic impactof potential Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF) events on
local communities including tourism-dependentregions;
(d) whether there are plans to extend similar mitigation measures to other
Himalayan Statesor regions facing GLOF risks in the future; and
(e) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS (SHRI
NITYANAND RAI):
(a) to (e): Strengthening of Early Warning Systems is prerequisite for preparedness
measures and is the most important element of entire cycle of disaster
management.
The Prime Minister has enunciated ten-point agenda on Disaster Risk Reduction
(DRR) during the Asian Ministerial Conference on Disaster Risk Reduction
(AMCDRR) held in New Delhi in November 2016. The all-inclusive agenda includes
the following: -
“Leverage technology to enhance the efficiency of disaster risk management
efforts.” and “Build on local capacity and initiative to enhance disaster risk
reduction”.
The Government effectively deploys technologies for improved early warning and
forecasting of disaster in the vulnerable areas. Central Government has designated
nodal agencies for early warning of different natural disasters.
01.04.2025 148
To promote the use of modern technologies and to strengthen the early warning
system for natural disasters, Ministry of Earth Sciences has launched a Multi-
faceted transformative approach namely “Mission Mausam” for the period 2024-
2026 with the goal of making India a “weather-ready and climate smart” nation.
Under the National Cyclone Risk Mitigation Project (NCRMP) Early Warning
Systems have been installed in the Coastal States, which have proved to be of
great help in alert dissemination to the coastal community during recent cyclones.
‘Common Alerting Protocol (CAP) based Integrated Alert System’ has been initiated
with an outlay of Rs. 354.83 Crore, for dissemination of geo targeted early
warnings/alerts related to disasters to the citizens of India for all 36 States/UTs
using various disseminating medium like SMS, TV, Radio, Indian Railways, Costal
Sirens, Cell broadcast, Internet (RSS feed & Browser Notification), Satellite
Receiver of GAGAN & NavIC etc., through integration of all alerting agencies, [India
Meteorological Department (IMD), Central Water Commission (CWC), Indian
National Centre for Ocean Information Services (INCOIS), Defence Geo-
informatics Research Establishment (DGRE), Geological Survey of India (GSI) and
Forest Survey of India (FSI)].
In CAP system, the alerts related to various disasters are generated by Alert
Generating Agencies like IMD, CWC, INCOIS, DGRE & FSI and moderated by
SDMAs of concern States/UTs. The alerts are sent to geo targeted areas in
regional languages. There is a web-based dashboard to disaster managers for
01.04.2025 149
approving/editing alerts and choosing media for dissemination. The system has
been used successfully in recent disasters. More than 4500 crore SMS alerts have
been disseminated so far using CAP.
National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has also initiated a project for
Pan India, end-to-end secure and foolproof Disaster Grade Cell Broadcasting
System (CBS) to improve faster dissemination of alert / early warning messages to
the citizen.
Defence Geoinformatics Research Establishment (DGRE), Chandigarh under
Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) is also the nodal
agency for studying and developing avalanche mitigation technologies. DGRE has
installed 72 Snow Meteorological Observatories and 45 Automated Weather
Stations (AWS).
India Metrological Department (IMD) issues regular and precise weather forecasts
& warning bulletins including for cyclones to all the affected/ likely affected States/
UTs.
IMD uses a suite of quality observations from Satellites, Radars and Conventional
& Automatic Weather Stations for monitoring of cyclones developing over the Bay
of Bengal and Arabian Sea. It includes INSAT 3D, 3DR and SCATSAT satellites,
Doppler Weather Radars (DWRs) along the coast and coastal Automated Weather
Stations (AWS), High wind speed recorders, Automatic Rain Gauges (ARGs),
Meteorological buoys and ships.
01.04.2025 150
NDMA also conducts capacity building programmes, organizes awareness
workshops and fosters community-based risk reduction strategies and also
trainings for monitoring and alert mechanism to ensure last mile connectivity.
Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology (WIHG) monitors the glaciers and provides
comprehensive analysis of factors that trigger hazards and its associated
downstream risks to significantly enhance early warning capabilities and disaster
preparedness. WIHG has prepared glacial lake inventories for Uttarakhand (2015)
and Himachal Pradesh (2018), identifying 1,266 lakes (7.6 km²) in Uttarakhand and
958 lakes (9.6 km²) in Himachal Pradesh.
Central Water Commission (CWC) monitors 902 Glacial lakes and water bodies, to
enable the detection of relative change in water spread areas of Glacial lakes and
water bodies as well as identifying those ones which have expanded substantially
during its monitoring months.
Central Government has approved National Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF)
Risk Mitigation Project (NGRMP) for its implementation in four states namely,
Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Sikkim and Uttarakhand at a financial
outlay of Rs. 150.00 crore.
NGRMP is aimed at reducing the risks associated with glacial lake outburst floods,
particularly in regions that are highly susceptible to such natural disasters. The
objectives of NGRMP project are:
01.04.2025 151
(i) Prevent loss of life and reduce economic loss and damage to critical
infrastructure due to GLOF and similar events.
(ii) Strengthen the early warning and monitoring capacities based on last mile
connectivity.
(iii) Strengthen scientific and technical capabilities in GLOF risk reduction and
mitigation at local levels through strengthening of local level institutions and
communities.
(iv) Use of indigenous knowledge and scientific cutting-edge mitigation
measures to reduce and mitigate GLOF risk.
NGRMP, approved by the Government, has one of its components as GLOF
monitoring and Early Warning Systems (EWS) including remote sensing data,
community involvement for monitoring, alerting / dissemination.
Two Automatic Weather Stations (AWS) have been installed in Sikkim with
further deployments of EWS planned in collaboration with C-DAC, ISRO and Space
Applications Centre, Ahmedabad to provide early warning to local communities in
case of any GLOF event.
CWC has finalized the criteria for Risk Indexing of Glacial Lakes offering a
structured approach for identifying and ranking such lakes based on their likelihood
of failure and potential damage they could cause in the event of GLOF.
A Committee on Disaster Risk Reduction (CoDRR) under NDMA involving
representatives from six Himalayan States / Union Territories and other
01.04.2025 152
Stakeholders, has identified a set of high risk glacial lakes for sending expeditions
to directly assess these lakes and prepare comprehensive mitigation strategies in
terms of setting up EWS / other structural and non-structural measures.
Subsequent to Teesta-III Hydroelectric dam collapse in October, 2023, CWC
has decided to review the design flood of all the existing and under construction
dams vulnerable to GLOFs to ensure their adequate spillway capacity for a
combination of Probable Maximum Flood / Standard Probable Flood and GLOF.
Further, GLOF Studies has been made mandatory for all new dams planned having
Glacial Lakes in their catchments.

PENDING ISSUES WITH STATES
4832. SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY:
Will the Minister of HOME AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government of Telangana sent any proposal to clear Pending
issues betweentwo States of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana including division of
Schedule IX – GovernmentCompanies and Corporations (Section 53, 68, 71 of
Act);
(b) the dispute regarding Schedule X Institutions (Section 75 of Act);
(c) the division of AP Bhavan (Section 48(1) of Act);
01.04.2025 156
(d) the claim of Andhra Pradesh for apportionment of assets of institutions not
mentionedanywhere in the Act; and
(e) if so, the details thereof and the action taken thereon?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS (SHRI
NITYANAND RAI):
(a) to (e): A reference was received from the Government of Telangana for
resolving the pending issues between States of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana
related to division of Schedule IX Government Companies and Corporations,
Schedule X Institutions and apportionment of assets of institutions not mentioned
anywhere in the Act.
There is disagreement between States of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana
regarding apportionment of assets and liabilities of Schedule IX
Companies/Corporations, Schedule X Training Institutions/Centres and Institutions
not mentioned anywhere in the Andhra Pradesh Reorganization Act, 2014.
The approach of the Central Government has consistently been that bilateral
issues can be resolved only with the cooperation of the State Governments
concerned and that the Central Government acts as facilitator for amicable
settlement of the disputes in a spirit of mutual accommodation and understanding.
The Ministry of Home Affairs reviews the progress of implementation of the various
provisions of the Act from time to time with Ministries/Departments concerned as
01.04.2025 157
well as representatives of Government of Andhra Pradesh and Government of
Telangana. So far, 36 such review meetings have been held.
The division of properties of Andhra Pradesh Bhavan, New Delhi between
Andhra Pradesh and Telangana has been resolved.
COORDINATION COMMITTEE FOR AFFIRMATIVE ACTION
4833. DR. ALOK KUMAR SUMAN:
Will the Minister of COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the action taken by the Coordination Committee for affirmative
action in theprivate sector since its inception in 2006;
(b) whether the Government has directed to the Apex Industry Associations for
considerationof affirmative action, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons
therefor;
(c) the details of the job provided to the SCs/STs by the Apex Industry Association
inoutsourcing/contractual employment along with the monitoring mechanism put in
place for the same; and
(d) whether it is a fact that employment of SCs/STs in the outsource/contractual
jobs is verymeagre due to lack of monitoring affirmative action and if so, the details
thereof?
01.04.2025 158
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY;
AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ELECTRONICS AND
INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (SHRI JITIN PRASADA):
(a) to (d): The Coordination Committee for Affirmative Action for SC/ST in the
Private Sector was set up by the Government in 2006. So far, 9 meetings of
Coordination Committee have been held. In the 1st Coordination Committee
meeting, it was stated that the best course for achieving progress on the issue of
affirmative action is through voluntary action by the industry itself. Accordingly, the
Apex Industry Associations namely Confederation of Indian Industry (CII),
Federation of Indian Chamber of Commerce and Industry (FICCI), Associated
Chambers of COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY of India (ASSOCHAM) and Dalit
Indian Chamber of COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY (DICCI) have prepared
Voluntary Code of Conduct (VCC) for their member companies centered around
education, employability and entrepreneurship and employment to achieve
inclusion.
Measures undertaken by the members of Industry Associations include
scholarships, vocational training, entrepreneurship development programmes,
coaching etc. The last updated information on Affirmative Action for SC/ST as
received by the apex Industry Associations i.e. CII, FICCI, ASSOCHAM and DICCI,
is given in the enclosed Statement.
01.04.2025 159
STATEMENT
Information on Affirmative Action for SC/ST in Private Sector by the Industry
Associations i.e ASSOCHAM, CII, FICCI and DICCI
CII upto FICCI upto ASSOCHAM DICCI upto
30.09.2024 31.12.2024 upto 31.03.2018
31.12.2020

IMPACT OF US RECIPROCAL TARIFFS
4834. SHRI MANISH TEWARI:
01.04.2025 160
Will the Minister of COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Union Government is aware of the recent announcement by the US
Government regarding the imposition of reciprocal tariffs on countries including
India, if so, the details thereof;
(b) details of the sectors of the Indian economy that are likely to be impacted by
these reciprocal tariffs, along with an assessment of the potential economic loss to
Indian industries and exporters;
(c) whether the Government has undertaken any negotiations or diplomatic
engagements to mitigate the adverse effects of these tariffs on Indian trade and
commerce, if so, the details thereof, if not, the reasons therefor;
(d) whether it is a fact that the proposed US tariffs could lead to a 0.1 to 0.6
percentage point reduction in India's GDP growth as reported by Goldman Sachs;
and
(e) what measures are being implemented by the Government to prevent similar
trade disruptions in the future?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY;
AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ELECTRONICS AND
INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (SHRI JITIN PRASADA):
(a) and (b): The U.S. has issued Memorandum on Reciprocal Trade and Tariffs on
February 13, 2025, wherein the Secretary of Commerce and United States Trade
Representative are to take necessary actions to investigate harm to U.S. from any
01.04.2025 161
non-reciprocal trade arrangements adopted by trading partners and provide a
report with detailed proposed remedies for each trading partner, based on which,
US action against relevant trading partner could, thereafter be undertaken under
any of the relevant US legislations. As on date, reciprocal tariffs have not been
applied by U.S. on India. Both countries have committed to negotiate a mutually
beneficial, multi-sector Bilateral Trade Agreement under which the countries would
focus on increasing market access, reducing tariff and non-tariff barriers, enhancing
supply chain integration and resolving key trade issues bilaterally.
(c) to (e): The Government of India continues to engage with the Government of
United States to negotiate a mutually beneficial, multi-sector Bilateral Trade
Agreement under which the countries would focus on increasing market access,
reducing tariff and non-tariff barriers, enhancing supply chain integration and
resolving key trade issues bilaterally.
EV CHARGING STATIONS UNDER FAME
4835. SHRI KRISHNA PRASAD TENNETI:
Will the Minister of HEAVY INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) the number of public Electric Vehicle (EV) charging stations installed across the
countryunder the Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid and Electric
Vehicles (FAME) Scheme, State-wise;
(b) the funds allocated, released and utilized for EV charging infrastructure
developmentduring the last five years, year-wise;
01.04.2025 162
(c) the percentage of renewable energy integration in EV charging stations and its
impact oncarbon footprint reduction; and
(d) the steps taken to encourage private sector participation, battery-swapping
technologiesand smart-grid solutions for sustainable EV adoption in the country?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HEAVY INDUSTRIES; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF STEEL (SHRI BHUPATHI RAJU
SRINIVASA VARMA):
(a): Details of Electric Vehicle Public Charging Stations (EVPCS ) installed under
FAME-II scheme is given in the enclosed Statement.
(b): Rs.633.44 crore have been utilized out of Rs.839 crore allocated under the
scheme. The details of year-wise funds released /utilised during the last five years
is as under:
Rs. crore
FY 2019- 2020- 2021- 2022- 2023- Total
20 21 22 23 24 Exp.
Grants released for 0 21.99 0 560 51.45 633.44
EVPCS to OMCs
(c) and (d): No such impact evaluation has been done by the Ministry of Heavy
Industries. Ministry of Power, "Guidelines for Installation and Operation of Electric
Vehicle Charging Infrastructure-2024" dated 17th September, 2024, encourage
charging during solar hours through concessional tariffs, integration of renewable
01.04.2025 163
energy in bus depots & promotion of solar carport etc. These guidelines emphasize
the role of public-private partnerships in expanding the EV charging infrastructure.
Setting up EV charging station has been designated as de-licensed activity,
simplifying the process for businesses. To make the land available at affordable
rates, it has been suggested that public land be made available to Government or
Public entities on a revenue-sharing model at Rs. 1 per kWh. For private entities,
the land may be made available through a competitive bidding process at a floor
price of Rs. 1 per kWh. Additionally, public tenders involving government land for
the establishment of BSS have been suggested to be kept technology agnostic.
State Governments have been advised to permit round-the-clock operations of
BSS.
